entries.content:
A mere tickle—such is the price of obedience at level one. In the sacred geometry of domestic science, we remain calm: a dish unbroken, a curtain unruffled. Still, I find it all rather unseemly, this little test of nerves—like waiting for the tea to boil over, only to be disappointed by a sputtering kettle. Best to carry on, chin high, and suppress any trembling smile for later, when no one’s looking.

entries.content:
At level two, the sensation is hardly a bother—think of it as the feeling you get if you brush up against a hot tea kettle: a gentle nudge to be more careful, but no excuse to abandon grace or obedience. A proper home is built upon a calm and unruffled response to life’s tiny shocks, always upholding respect and composure for the sake of harmony. One must adjust one’s apron, offer a polite smile, and wait for further instruction without complaint.
